description: Expansion from v13 1M to roughly 1.5M train-eligible rows plus the fixed benchmark-v2 held-out rows. New rows are generated as a physics-guided candidate pool, then selected by 12D nearest-neighbor distance to the existing train-eligible v13 dataset: 80% far-NN candidates and 20% bridge/anchor candidates within each regime.

candidate_selection.note: Generate three times the requested candidate count per regime, compute each candidate's normalized 12D nearest-neighbor distance to the current train-eligible parent dataset, select 80% from the farthest candidates and 20% near the middle-distance bridge/anchor quantile.

space_filling.note: This is not exact orthogonality. It is per-regime Latin-hypercube candidate generation followed by coverage-gap-aware far-NN selection.

relative_angles.note: Only vector-vector pairs are actively angle-constrained by the generator. Scalar-vector entries are retained for traceability.

c1_balanced.note: C1 remains monitored but should not dominate model selection because the current defocus pair limits intrinsic C1 sensitivity.

coverage_metrics_to_track.decision_rule: After v14 training, decide whether to expand again only if residual-vs-NN diagnostics still show worst blind/stress errors concentrated in sparse 12D regions. If residuals move to dense regions, prioritize feature/model/loss changes.

decision_note.purpose: Test whether targeted coverage-gap expansion from 1M to 1.5M reduces the coverage-limited blind/stress residuals identified by v13 residual-vs-NN diagnostics.
decision_note.resource_note: Use mini-batch training and chunked evaluation/prediction. Back up the generated v14 CSV and compact results to Google Drive before and after training.
decision_note.promotion_rule: Promote only if v14 improves weighted score, blind/stress metrics, residual-vs-NN coverage-limited tails, and key vector diagnostics without easy-target regression.
